binaries.title: netio.sys - WIL feature-staging graduation and compiler inlining

changed_functions.name: NsiEnumerateObjectsAllParametersEx
key_changes: Patched build inlines the out-of-line context-release chain that unpatched reaches via NsipDereferenceNmpContext (0x140030330) on its LABEL_55 release path

The inlined chain corresponds to NsipDereferenceNmpContext -> NmrClientDetachProviderComplete -> NmrpGetBindingFromHandle -> NmrpDetachProviderComplete -> NmrpDereferenceBinding, all of which exist in the unpatched build
The object-magic check (0x6c43 == 27715), the interlocked refcount operation, both KeBugCheckEx(0x125, ...) assertions, and the NmrpDereferenceBinding cleanup are present in BOTH builds
changed_functions.security_notes: No security-relevant change. The earlier claim that an unvalidated iterator (sub_1400759d0) was replaced by a validating one (sub_1400742a0) is not supported: neither address exists in either binary, and there is no object-iteration while-loop in this function. The only real difference is that the patched build inlines the reference-release chain that the unpatched build calls out-of-line. Every lifecycle assertion credited to the patch already exists unpatched: the magic-value check with KeBugCheckEx(0x125,1) in NmrpGetBindingFromHandle (0x14004BB54), and the interlocked refcount check with KeBugCheckEx(0x125,3) plus NmrpDereferenceBinding in NmrpDetachProviderComplete (0x140048C84). Direction: no check added, no check removed.
changed_functions.assembly_diff: Unpatched callee NmrpGetBindingFromHandle @0x14004BB54: 0x14004BB60 mov edx,6C43h; 0x14004BB65 cmp ax,dx; 0x14004BBA5 call KeBugCheckEx. Unpatched callee NmrpDetachProviderComplete @0x140048C84: 0x140048C8D lock xadd [rcx+50h],eax; 0x140048CB1 call KeBugCheckEx; 0x140048CBE call NmrpDereferenceBinding. Patched NsiEnumerateObjectsAllParametersEx @0x14002F5C0 inlines the same: 0x14002FF29 lock xadd [r13+0],eax; 0x14002FFB8 mov ecx,6C43h; 0x14002FFC3 lock xadd [r8+4],r12d; 0x14002FFEC call KeBugCheckEx; 0x14002FFFD call NmrpDereferenceBinding.

changed_functions.name: DeleteFilterFromIndex
key_changes: Acquisition of gLayerStatsLock and the AreAllIpv4InLayersEmpty update are gated in the unpatched build behind WIL staged features Feature_Firewall_Rules_Filters_Performance_Counters and Feature_Netsec_BugFixes_25C_Loopback_Checksum_Injection
Patched build graduates the feature to always-on: gLayerStatsLock is always taken around the statistics update (WfpAcquireFastWriteLock / WfpReleaseFastWriteLock)
The statistics counter decrement (_InterlockedDecrement) is atomic in both builds
changed_functions.security_notes: No security-relevant change. The gate is a WIL feature flag (Feature_Firewall_Rules_Filters_Performance_Counters__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline), not a global data_14009b248 flag, and the helper sub_140044424 does not exist. The lock protects layer-statistics counters, not the filter object; the counter update is atomic in both builds. This is a staged-feature rollout of performance counters, not a fix for a race on shared filter data.
changed_functions.assembly_diff: Unpatched DeleteFilterFromIndex @0x140040F80: 0x14004117A mov eax, Feature_Netsec_BugFixes_25C_Loopback_Checksum_Injection__private_featureState; 0x140041189 call Feature_Netsec_BugFixes_25C_Loopback_Checksum_Injection__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline; 0x1400411B3 call Feature_Firewall_Rules_Filters_Performance_Counters__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline; 0x1400411E0 call WfpAcquireFastWriteLock; 0x140041237 call NdisReleaseRWLock.

changed_functions.name: NsipValidateEnumerateObjectsAllParametersRequest
key_changes: Parameter-buffer size checks (request fields at 0x38/0x40, 0x48/0x50, 0x58/0x60 vs the module descriptor's expected sizes) are gated in the unpatched build behind WIL staged feature Feature_TCPIP_2025_Wave2_NsiVersioning
Patched build performs the same checks unconditionally (feature graduated to always-on)
changed_functions.security_notes: No security-relevant change. The gate is the WIL feature Feature_TCPIP_2025_Wave2_NsiVersioning__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline, not a global data_14009b200 flag or sub_14005ea18. The checks validate a new versioned request format; graduating the feature to always-on enables them. This is a staged feature rollout, not the removal of a gate on validation of an already-shipping path.

changed_functions.name: sub_140030ed0
key_changes: Same WIL feature-staging pattern as NsipValidateEnumerateObjectsAllParametersRequest: validation gated behind a Feature_TCPIP_2025_Wave2_* staged feature in the unpatched build, made unconditional in the patched build
changed_functions.security_notes: No security-relevant change. Feature-staging graduation, same nature as NsipValidateEnumerateObjectsAllParametersRequest.

changed_functions.name: sub_140013030
key_changes: Inline RWLock/statistics code extracted to a helper function

changed_functions.name: FeInit
key_changes: WFP filter-engine initialization refactored; conditional (WIL-gated) init replaced with unconditional init
changed_functions.security_notes: Initialization function; the changes are refactoring plus feature-staging graduation applied at load time. Not reachable as an attacker primitive.

changed_functions.name: NetioLookupForwardFlow
key_changes: Loop iteration restructured; variable renaming. No logical impact.

changed_functions.name: NsiGetParameterEx
key_changes: Same WIL feature-staging pattern (gated validation made unconditional) as the other NSI request functions
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-staging graduation, same nature as the other NSI functions. Not a security fix.

summary: No security-relevant change was delivered by this patch. The three areas originally flagged are explained by compiler inlining and WIL feature-staging graduation, with all relevant checks present in BOTH builds. (1) NsiEnumerateObjectsAllParametersEx (unpatched 0x14002F8D0, patched 0x14002F5C0): the patched build inlines the out-of-line reference-release chain (NsipDereferenceNmpContext -> NmrClientDetachProviderComplete -> NmrpGetBindingFromHandle -> NmrpDetachProviderComplete -> NmrpDereferenceBinding). The object-magic check (0x6c43), interlocked refcount operation, both KeBugCheckEx(0x125,...) assertions, and NmrpDereferenceBinding cleanup already exist in the unpatched build inside those callees; no check was added or removed. The functions sub_1400759d0/sub_1400742a0 cited by the original draft do not exist in either binary. (2) DeleteFilterFromIndex: the layer-statistics lock is gated behind the WIL feature Feature_Firewall_Rules_Filters_Performance_Counters in the unpatched build and graduated to always-on in the patched build; the counter decrement is atomic in both. (3) NsipValidateEnumerateObjectsAllParametersRequest: buffer-size validation is gated behind the WIL feature Feature_TCPIP_2025_Wave2_NsiVersioning in the unpatched build and made unconditional in the patched build. An independent content-matched diff shows the remaining changed set is dominated by WPP/ETW tracing churn, WIL feature-staging plumbing, new staged features (audit mode, NSI versioning), and function relocation/inlining; no omitted security-relevant fix was found.
